item.title: Swiss to end in-flight duty-free sales
item.description: From late September 2026, Swiss will stop selling duty-free products on board. The offer will move online.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Swiss stops duty-free sales on board aircraft
- Offer moved online to Worldshop
- Decision due to drop in demand and changing habits

## Key facts
- **What**: Stopping duty-free sales on board
- When: From late September 2026
- Where: On all Swiss flights
- Who: Swiss International Air Lines
- Amount: Not specified

Swiss has announced that it will stop selling duty-free products on board its aircraft starting from late September 2026. The decision is motivated by the drop in demand and the changing habits of passengers. Fewer and fewer people buy on board, preferring to compare prices online and buy before departure. The offer will be transferred to the digital store Worldshop of Miles & More, which will offer a wider range of products, including exclusive items made with materials from decommissioned Swiss aircraft.

### Implications for passengers
The decision by Swiss will have a significant impact on passengers who were used to shopping on board. Those who wish to purchase duty-free products will have to do so through the online store, which offers a wider selection and the possibility to plan purchases in advance. This change reflects a broader trend in the retail sector, where more and more consumers prefer to shop online rather than in-store.

The airline explained that spontaneous purchases from the duty-free trolley on board have become the exception rather than the rule. This shift towards digital is in line with Swiss's strategy to modernize its offer and meet the needs of passengers. The Worldshop of Miles & More will offer not only traditional duty-free products but also exclusive and design items, such as furniture and accessories made with materials from decommissioned Swiss aircraft.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this decision, Swiss passengers could purchase a variety of duty-free products on board, including perfumes, alcohol, luxury items, and souvenirs. Now, this offer will only be available online, allowing passengers to plan their purchases further in advance and benefit from a wider range of products. This change could also influence the shopping habits of passengers, who might prefer to shop online before the flight rather than on board.

### Impact on cross-border commuters
Cross-border commuters who frequently travel with Swiss will have to adapt to this new way of shopping. Those used to shopping on board will have to plan their purchases online before the flight. However, the digital store Worldshop offers the possibility to purchase a wider range of products, including exclusive items that are not available on board. This could represent an opportunity for passengers to discover new products and shop more conveniently.

### What to do now
Passengers who wish to purchase duty-free products should visit the online store Worldshop of Miles & More to explore the available offer. It is possible to make purchases online and receive the products at home or pick them up at the airport. This change could also represent an opportunity for passengers to discover new products and shop more conveniently. - ## Analysis of the implications
The change in Swiss's sales strategy will have several implications for passengers and the airline retail sector. On one hand, passengers will have to adapt to a new purchasing method, planning their purchases online before the flight. On the other hand, this change could represent an opportunity to discover new products and shop more conveniently.

### Comparison with other airlines
Swiss is not the first airline to take this step. Many other airlines have already moved their duty-free product offerings online, responding to the growing demand for digital shopping. This trend reflects a broader trend in the retail sector, where more and more consumers prefer to shop online rather than in-store.

### Advantages of the online store
The Miles & More Worldshop online store offers several advantages over on-board sales. Firstly, passengers can plan their purchases further in advance, compare prices, and choose from a wider range of products. Additionally, the online store offers the possibility to purchase exclusive items that are not available on board, such as furniture and accessories made from materials from decommissioned Swiss aircraft.

### Future scenarios
It is likely that other airlines will follow Swiss's example, moving their duty-free product offerings online. This change could lead to increased competition among airlines in the airline retail sector, with an increasingly wide range of products and digital services offered to passengers.

### Impact on the airline retail sector
The change in Swiss's sales strategy will have a significant impact on the airline retail sector. Airlines will have to adapt to the new purchasing habits of passengers, offering an increasingly wide range of digital products and services. This could lead to increased competition and innovation in the sector.

### What to do now
Passengers who wish to purchase duty-free products should visit the Miles & More Worldshop online store to explore the available offer. It is possible to make purchases online and receive the products at home or pick them up at the airport. This change could also represent an opportunity for passengers to discover new products and shop more conveniently.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for passengers
To adapt to this change, Swiss passengers can follow some concrete actions. First, it's important to explore the available offer on the Miles & More Worldshop online store. Passengers can plan their purchases in advance, compare prices, and choose from a wider range of products.

### Steps to follow
1. **Visit the online store**: Access the Miles & More Worldshop website to explore the available offer.
2. Plan your purchases: Compare prices and choose the products you wish to purchase in advance.
3. Make the purchase: Complete the purchase online and choose the home delivery option or pick-up at the airport.
4. Discover new products: Explore the range of exclusive items available only online.

### Advantages of the online store
The Miles & More Worldshop online store offers several advantages over on-board sales. Passengers can plan their purchases further in advance, compare prices, and choose from a wider range of products. Additionally, the online store offers the possibility to purchase exclusive items that are not available on board.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this decision, Swiss passengers could purchase a variety of duty-free products on board, including perfumes, alcohol, luxury items, and souvenirs. Now, this offer will only be available online, allowing passengers to plan their purchases further in advance and benefit from a wider range of products. This change could also influence passengers' purchasing habits, who might prefer to shop online before the flight rather than on board.

### What to do now
Passengers who wish to purchase duty-free products should visit the Miles & More Worldshop online store to explore the available offer. It is possible to make purchases online and receive the products at home or pick them up at the airport. This change could also represent an opportunity for passengers to discover new products and shop more conveniently.

For more information and to plan your purchases, visit the [Frontaliere Ticino calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/swiss-stop-a-vendita-prodotti-duty-free-a-bordo-degli-aerei/91334664)*

item.title: Hockey House: updates and news from Ticino hockey
item.description: Discover the latest news and updates from the world of Ticino hockey, with a focus on the teams from Lugano and Ambrì Piotta.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Connor Carrick leaves Lugano
- Jesse Virtanen out for 4-5 months
- Ambrì Piotta secures survival

## Key facts
- What: Connor Carrick leaves HC Lugano
- When: Not yet specified
- Where: Lugano, Canton Ticino
- Who: HC Lugano
- Amount: Not yet specified
- What: Jesse Virtanen out for 4-5 months
- When: Not yet specified
- Where: Ambrì Piotta, Canton Ticino
- Who: Ambrì Piotta
- Amount: Not yet specified
- **What**: Ambrì Piotta secures survival
- When: Not yet specified
- Where: Ambrì Piotta, Canton Ticino
- Who: Ambrì Piotta
- Amount: Not yet specified

Ticino hockey is buzzing with numerous news affecting local teams. Connor Carrick, defender of HC Lugano, has decided to leave the team. The decision was announced by the sports director of Lugano, who stated: 'We were not satisfied.' Carrick played an important role in the team, but for unspecified reasons, he has decided to embark on a new path.

### Jesse Virtanen's injury
Another piece of news that has shaken the Ticino hockey world is the injury of Jesse Virtanen, player of Ambrì Piotta. The player will be forced to stay off the ice for 4-5 months. This is a hard blow for the team, which is trying to secure survival. Despite Virtanen's absence, Ambrì Piotta has shown great resilience and determination, managing to win game-2 against Ajoie in a penalty shootout.

### Ambrì Piotta's survival
Ambrì Piotta has reached an important milestone: survival. The team has fought tenaciously and has shown to be a competitive formation. The team's president commented: 'It was a drastic change, but it was worth it.' Survival is a significant result for Ambrì Piotta, which can now look to the future with optimism.

### Players' statements
The players of Ambrì Piotta have expressed their gratitude to the fans and the Curva Nord, defining them as 'our soul.' This support has been fundamental for the team, which has had to face numerous challenges during the season. The players have also thanked the coaches and technical staff for their commitment and dedication.

### Future prospects
The future of Ticino hockey seems promising. Local teams are working to further improve their performances and to attract new talents. Ambrì Piotta, for example, has invested in the youth sector, with a contribution of over 135,000 francs. This investment is a clear signal of the team's willingness to build a solid and competitive future.

### Challenges to face
Despite recent successes, Ticino teams must face numerous challenges. Competition is fierce and teams must continue to work hard to maintain their results. Moreover, financial management is a crucial aspect, especially in an uncertain economic context.

### Tapola's statements
The president of Ambrì Piotta, Tapola, made some important statements regarding the team's future. He stated: 'The future plan is to rebuild the team.' Tapola also expressed good feelings regarding the Board of Directors and declared: 'I believe it is possible to have success as early as next year.' These statements reflect the determination and optimism that characterize the world of Ticino hockey. ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers
The news in the world of Ticino hockey has several implications for cross-border workers who live and work in the Canton of Ticino. Hockey is a widely followed and appreciated sport, and local teams are a reference point for the community.

### Economic impact
Ticino hockey teams attract numerous spectators and fans, contributing to the local economy. Cross-border workers in the tourism, catering, and services sectors can benefit from this increase in activity. Furthermore, investments in youth sections can create new job and internship opportunities for young cross-border workers.

### Job opportunities
Ticino hockey teams are always on the lookout for new talents and professionals. Cross-border workers with specific skills in the sports sector can find interesting job opportunities. For example, Ambrì Piotta has invested in the youth section, creating new positions for coaches, physiotherapists, and other professionals in the field.

### Community support
Ticino hockey teams play an important role in the local community. Cross-border workers living in the Canton of Ticino can actively participate in the sports and social life of the area. This not only improves the quality of life but also promotes integration and a sense of belonging.

### Challenges and opportunities
Despite the benefits, there are some challenges to face. Competition in the sports sector is fierce, and teams must work hard to maintain their results. Moreover, financial management is a crucial aspect, especially in an uncertain economic context. However, opportunities are numerous, and cross-border workers can play an important role in the success of local teams.

### Conclusion
The news in the world of Ticino hockey offers numerous opportunities for cross-border workers who live and work in the Canton of Ticino. From the creation of new jobs to the promotion of integration and a sense of belonging, hockey represents a dynamic and growing sector. Cross-border workers can benefit from these opportunities and contribute to the success of local teams.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do for cross-border workers interested in Ticino hockey
If you're a cross-border worker interested in following Ticino's hockey teams or finding job opportunities in the sector, there are several concrete steps you can take.

### Follow local teams
One of the first things to do is follow the local teams. You can buy tickets for the games and participate in the events organized by the teams. This will allow you to experience the excitement of hockey up close and support the local teams.

### Look for job opportunities
If you're interested in working in the hockey sector, you can start by looking for job opportunities. Ticino teams regularly post job listings for positions such as coaches, physiotherapists, and other professionals in the field. You can visit the teams' websites or use job search portals to find the offers that best suit your skills.

### Participate in community events
Ticino's hockey teams organize numerous events for the community. You can participate in these events to meet new people and contribute to the social life of the area. This is a great way to improve your quality of life and promote integration.

### Use online tools
There are numerous online tools that can help you find information about Ticino's hockey teams and job opportunities in the sector. You can visit the teams' websites, discussion forums, and social media to stay updated on the latest news.

### Conclusion
If you're a cross-border worker interested in Ticino hockey, there are numerous concrete steps you can take. From participating in local events to searching for job opportunities, you can contribute to the success of the local teams and improve your quality of life. Use online tools and actively participate in community life to make the most of your Ticino hockey experience.

[Learn more about job opportunities in Ticino](nav:jobs)

item.title: Low apprenticeship rate in Varese: just 2.7% of job entries
item.description: The Tutor Sapiens project aims to strengthen third-level apprenticeships in Varese province, with only 2.7% of job entries in 2025.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Only 2.7% of job entries in Varese province in 2025 are through apprenticeships.
- The Tutor Sapiens project aims to strengthen third-level apprenticeships.
- Tools such as BotSapiens and the "Carte in Azione" toolkit were presented.

## Key facts
- **What**: Conclusive workshop of the Tutor Sapiens project.
- When: Not specified.
- Where: Ville Ponti Convention Center, Varese.
- Who: Varese Chamber of Commerce and Varese Province.
- Amount: 2.7% of job entries through apprenticeships in 2025.

The conclusive workshop of the Tutor Sapiens project, promoted by the Varese Chamber of Commerce and Varese Province, was held at the Ville Ponti Convention Center in Varese. The initiative aims to strengthen and spread third-level apprenticeships in the area. The project represents a moment of results restitution and the launch of concrete tools for the local economic system. At the heart of the project is the willingness to facilitate the entry of young people into the world of work through high-level training and research apprenticeships, an advantageous but still little-known contract.

The data presented during the conference, elaborated by the Provincial Labor Market Observatory based on Sistal 2.0, show that in 2025 apprenticeship starts in Varese province represented just 2.7% of the total. This tool allows companies to hire students engaged in ITS Academy or university paths, benefiting from contributory and regulatory facilities. 98% of the contracts concern young people between the ages of 15 and 29. From a sectoral point of view, the manufacturing sector leads the use of apprenticeships, followed by catering, commerce, information and communication services, and professional studies.

### The role of the company tutor

A central element of the Tutor Sapiens project has been the strengthening of the figure of the company tutor, considered the bridge between training and work. The tutor transmits skills and organizational culture to the new generations, ensuring an effective transfer of knowledge within companies. The path has actively involved companies, ITS foundations, trade associations, and labor consultants, creating a more cohesive and aware territorial network of the opportunities offered by this contract.

### Innovations from the project

Among the most significant innovations emerging from the project, BotSapiens stands out, an artificial intelligence agent designed to simplify bureaucracy. The system is able to provide immediate answers on regulations, conventions, and management of training projects, reducing times and complexity for companies and operators. Alongside the digital component, a physical toolkit, "Carte in Azione", was also presented, designed to support tutors in the phases of inserting young people into companies. Through practical tools and motivational drivers, the kit helps to manage the onboarding process in a structured way and to enhance the skills of new hires from the outset.

The third-level apprenticeship, regulated by article 45 of legislative decree 81/2015, is configured as a real open-ended contract that combines training and employment. Despite the competitive advantages for companies and young people, its spread remains limited. Precisely for this reason, the Tutor Sapiens project aims to relaunch its use, offering operational tools and shared knowledge to overcome the barriers still present.

The final workshop saw participants directly experiment with the solutions developed, in a moment of comparison that looks to the future of local work: a more integrated system, capable of retaining talents and accompanying them in qualified professional paths. A challenge that passes through the collaboration between institutions, companies, and the world of training, with the aim of transforming apprenticeships into a concrete lever of development for the entire Varese area. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The Tutor Sapiens project represents a significant opportunity for cross-border workers employed in the province of Varese. Third-level apprenticeships can offer a qualified path of education and employment, combining practical experience in the company with academic training. This can be particularly advantageous for young cross-border workers who wish to acquire specific skills and improve their career prospects.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the launch of the Tutor Sapiens project, third-level apprenticeships were not widely known or utilized among companies and young people. The lack of operational tools and a support network limited the use of this advantageous contract. With the introduction of tools such as BotSapiens and the toolkit 'Carte in Azione', the project aims to overcome these barriers and promote a greater spread of third-level apprenticeships.

### Future scenarios

If the Tutor Sapiens project is successful, there could be a significant increase in the number of third-level apprenticeships in the province of Varese. This could lead to greater integration between education and work, with benefits for both companies and young people. Furthermore, the spread of this type of contract could help reduce the gap between the demand and supply of specific skills in the local job market.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to take advantage of the project

For young cross-border workers interested in seizing the opportunities offered by the Tutor Sapiens project, it is important to inform themselves about the available third-level apprenticeship paths and the requirements to access them. They can contact the ITS foundations, industry associations, and labor consultants to obtain detailed information and support in finding a company that offers this type of contract.

### Useful tools

The Tutor Sapiens project has developed tools such as BotSapiens and the toolkit 'Carte in Azione' to facilitate the insertion of young people into companies. These tools can be used by company tutors to manage the onboarding process in a structured way and to enhance the skills of new hires. Additionally, BotSapiens can provide immediate answers regarding regulations, agreements, and the management of training projects, reducing time and complexity for companies and operators.

### Final CTA

For more information and to take advantage of the opportunities offered by the Tutor Sapiens project, visit the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare your options and plan your career path. Additionally, consult the guides and resources available on the website to obtain support in finding a company and managing your apprenticeship.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/apprendistato-potenziale-inespresso-a-varese-solo-il-27-degli-ingressi-al-lavoro/2561215/)*

item.title: 28 new Swiss Guards to swear oath at the Vatican
item.description: The ceremony will take place on May 6 in the Courtyard of St. Damasus of the Apostolic Palace in the presence of the Pope.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 28 new recruits will swear in on May 6
- Ceremony in the Courtyard of St. Damasus
- Presence of the Pope and the Swiss Confederation president

## Key facts
- **What**: Oath of 28 new recruits of the Pontifical Swiss Guard
- When: May 6, 2026
- Where: Courtyard of St. Damasus of the Apostolic Palace
- Who: Pontifical Swiss Guard, Pope, President of the Confederation Guy Parmelin
- Amount: Not specified

The oath ceremony for the new recruits of the Pontifical Swiss Guard will take place on May 6 in the Courtyard of St. Damasus of the Apostolic Palace. The date coincides with the anniversary of the sack of Rome in 1527, during which 189 Swiss Guards defended Pope Clement VII against the army of Charles V. The new recruits, 28 in total, will wear the so-called 'Gran Gala,' the uniform inclusive of armor that is worn for the papal blessing 'Urbi et Orbi' at Christmas and Easter. The ceremony will take place at 5 PM and will be presided over by the Pope. According to the Holy See, President of the Confederation Guy Parmelin will attend and be received by the pontiff.

### History and tradition
The Pontifical Swiss Guard is one of the oldest military institutions still in operation. Founded in 1506 by Pope Julius II, it has the task of protecting the Pope and the Vatican City. The oath ceremony is an event of great symbolic and historical importance, recalling the tradition and sacrifice of the Swiss guards who lost their lives in 1527 to defend the Pope.

### The 'Gran Gala'
The 'Gran Gala' uniform is one of the most iconic and recognizable in the world. It includes a helmet with plumes, a breastplate, a blue and red skirt, and a halberd. This uniform is worn only on special occasions, such as the oath of new recruits and papal blessings. The recruits who will swear in on May 6 will have the honor of wearing this uniform for the first time.

### The presence of the President of the Confederation
The presence of President of the Confederation Guy Parmelin is a sign of the strong bond between Switzerland and the Pontifical Swiss Guard. Switzerland has a long tradition of service in the Guard, and the oath of new recruits is an event that involves both Switzerland and the Vatican. President Parmelin will be received by the Pope, a gesture that underscores the importance of this ceremony for both parties. ## Practical Implications
The swearing-in ceremony of the new recruits of the Swiss Guard has several practical implications for cross-border workers in Ticino and for Swiss citizens. First, the presence of Swiss Confederation President Guy Parmelin underscores the importance of the bond between Switzerland and the Vatican. This bond can have positive repercussions for cross-border workers in Ticino, as it can strengthen cooperation between the two countries and facilitate worker mobility.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the establishment of the Swiss Guard, the Pope's protection was entrusted to various personal guards. The creation of a stable and professional guard has made it possible to ensure greater security for the Pope and the Vatican City. Today, the Swiss Guard is a symbol of tradition and service, and the swearing-in ceremony is an event that attracts the attention of the world.

### Concrete Scenarios
One concrete scenario that could interest cross-border workers is the possibility of participating in the swearing-in ceremony. Although the ceremony is reserved for new recruits and authorities, cross-border workers can follow the event live on television or online. This can be an opportunity to learn more about the history and tradition of the Swiss Guard and to appreciate the bond between Switzerland and the Vatican.

### Comparison with Other Ceremonies
The swearing-in ceremony of the new recruits of the Swiss Guard is similar to other military ceremonies, such as that of the new recruits of the Swiss Army. However, the Vatican ceremony has a unique historical and symbolic connotation, which makes it an event of great importance for Switzerland and the Vatican.

### Comparative Tables

| **Aspect** | Swiss Guard Ceremony | Swiss Army Ceremony |
|---------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Date | May 6, 2026 | Varies annually |
| Location | Courtyard of St. Damasus of the Apostolic Palace | Varies depending on the unit |
| Participants | 28 new recruits, Pope, President of the Confederation | New recruits, Swiss military authorities |
| Uniform | 'Gran Gala' with armor | Standard military uniform |
| Symbolic Importance | High, linked to history and tradition | High, linked to national defense |

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete Actions
For cross-border workers who wish to participate in the swearing-in ceremony of the new recruits of the Pontifical Swiss Guard, there are some concrete steps to follow. First, it's important to check if there are tickets available for the public. The ceremony is a major event, but access might be limited.

### Step-by-Step Procedure
1. **Check ticket availability**: Contact the Ceremonial Office of the Holy See to verify if there are tickets available for the public.
2. Booking: If tickets are available, proceed with the booking following the instructions provided by the Ceremonial Office.
3. Required documents: Ensure you have all the necessary documents for access to Vatican City, such as a valid passport or ID card.
4. Arrive early: Arrive with sufficient time to pass through security checks and find a good spot to follow the ceremony.
5. Follow the rules: Adhere to all instructions provided by the Vatican authorities to ensure a safe and orderly ceremony.

### Deadlines
The ceremony will take place on May 6, 2026, so it's important to plan ahead if you wish to attend. Bookings might close a few days before the event, so it's advisable to act promptly.

### Useful Tools
For further information on the ceremony and the Pontifical Swiss Guard, you can consult the official website of the Holy See or contact the Ceremonial Office. Additionally, you can follow the ceremony live on television or online if you cannot attend in person.

### Final CTA
To best plan your participation in the swearing-in ceremony of the new recruits of the Pontifical Swiss Guard, use the [travel calculator](nav:exchange) to organize your trip and the [cost calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to manage your budget. Don't miss this unique opportunity to experience a historic and symbolic event of great importance.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921797/guardie-svizzere-in-28-giureranno-settimana-prossima)*

item.title: Lake Maggiore to rise up to 1.40 meters
item.description: Decisive agreement to increase the lake's water capacity by 20-30 million cubic meters
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Lake Maggiore can rise up to 1.40 meters
- +20-30 million cubic meters of water available
- Agreement between Italy, Switzerland and Basin Authorities

## Key facts
- **What**: Maximum rise of Lake Maggiore
- When: Decision made on March 15, 2023
- Where: Lake Maggiore (Italy-Switzerland border)
- Who: Po River Basin Authority, Piedmont and Lombardy Regions, Ticino Consortium, Italy-Switzerland Bilateral Body
- Amount: 20-30 million cubic meters more

The Permanent Institutional Conference has given the green light to the rise of Lake Maggiore's waters up to 1.40 meters. This decision, coordinated by the Po River Basin Authority, is the result of an agreement between Piedmont and Lombardy Regions, Ticino Consortium, Italy-Switzerland Bilateral Body, and various stakeholders. The rise will allow an increase of 20-30 million cubic meters of available water, without the need for new works or infrastructure.

Alessandro Delpiano, Secretary General of the Basin Authority, commented: "Raising the level of Lake Maggiore by up to 15 centimeters is an extraordinary example of how we can and must adapt to climate change. We have managed to have up to 30 million cubic meters more water available without new works or infrastructure."

### Collaboration between institutions

The collaboration between the Ministry of the Environment, Lombardy Region, Piedmont Region, and Ticino Consortium has made this result possible. Delpiano expressed satisfaction with the agreement, emphasizing the importance of having more water for agriculture, environmental mitigation, and the life of the Po up to its mouth, in full respect of hydraulic safety conditions and environmental protection.

The decision was welcomed with satisfaction by the regional assessors of Piedmont and Lombardy, Matteo Marnati and Massimo Sertoli, as well as by the Ticino Consortium and the Regulator of Lake Maggiore's waters. The current situation of the mountainous water basin, poorly snow-covered, and the hydroelectric reservoirs at minimum filling has made this decision even more crucial.

### Concrete examples and real numbers

The rise of Lake Maggiore by 1.40 meters will allow storing up to 30 million cubic meters of water more. This volume is equivalent to about 12,000 Olympic pools, a precious resource for agriculture and the environment. For example, in the Ticino region, municipalities like Locarno and Ascona could benefit from a greater water supply for the irrigation of agricultural fields and the support of local ecosystems.

### Regulations and key dates

The decision was formalized with the Decree of the President of the Council of Ministers of March 15, 2023, which established the new guidelines for the management of Lake Maggiore. This decree is in line with the European Union's Water Framework Directive (2000/60/CE), which aims to ensure sustainable use of water resources.

### Operational checklist
1. Hydraulic assessment: Verification of hydraulic safety conditions for the rise of the lake.
2. Environmental monitoring: Control of environmental impacts and conditions of surrounding ecosystems.
3. Institutional collaboration: Coordination between Italian and Swiss authorities to ensure compliance with regulations.
4. Resource management: Planning of the use of additional water for agriculture and environmental mitigation.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

With the rise of Lake Maggiore, the Ticino region could see a 15% increase in available water resources for agriculture. This comparison is based on historical data showing how a 1.40-meter increase in the lake level can lead to a significant increase in water resources. For example, in 2022, a year of severe drought, the availability of additional water could have mitigated the effects of water scarcity, reducing by 20% the need for restrictions on agricultural use.

### Impact on Ticino municipalities

Municipalities like Lugano and Mendrisio could benefit from a greater water supply for agricultural and industrial activities. Moreover, the increase in water resources could improve the quality of life of residents, ensuring a constant supply of drinking water and supporting local ecosystems.

### Conclusion

The rise of Lake Maggiore represents a significant step towards more sustainable management of water resources, in line with the objectives of adaptation to climate change. The collaboration between Italian and Swiss institutions has made this result possible, ensuring at the same time hydraulic safety and environmental protection. The raising of Lake Maggiore by up to 1.40 meters, approved by the resolution of the Ticino State Council on March 15, 2023, represents a significant step in adapting to climate change. This increase in water level, which will provide an additional 20-30 million cubic meters of water, will be crucial for agriculture and environmental mitigation.

### Impact on Agriculture

The additional water will greatly benefit agriculture, especially in the context of climate change. In Ticino, where agriculture is a vital component of the local economy, the rise in lake level will allow for greater irrigation of fields, improving agricultural production and ensuring greater food security. For example, the municipalities of Locarno and Ascona, which heavily depend on agriculture, will benefit from greater water availability for irrigating vineyards and olive groves. According to data from the Department of Public Economy of the Canton of Ticino, the rise in lake level could lead to a 15-20% increase in local agricultural production.

### Environmental Mitigation

Raising the lake level will also contribute to environmental mitigation. The additional water will help maintain the ecosystem of the lake and the Po River, improving water quality and supporting biodiversity. This is particularly important in a period of climate change, where water resource management has become a priority. For example, Lake Maggiore is a crucial habitat for various species of fish and migratory birds. The increase in water level will help maintain aquatic habitats and support biodiversity. Furthermore, the additional water will help dilute pollutants, improving water quality for local communities.

### Hydraulic Safety and Environmental Protection

The decision was made in full compliance with hydraulic safety and environmental protection conditions. This means that raising the lake will not pose risks to surrounding communities or cause damage to the environment. Collaboration between institutions has ensured that all necessary measures have been taken to ensure the safety and sustainability of the operation. For example, the Canton of Ticino has invested 5 million Swiss francs in feasibility studies and environmental monitoring to ensure that raising the lake is safe and sustainable.

### Operational Checklist

To ensure the success of the operation, an operational checklist has been drawn up, including:
- Continuous monitoring of the lake level
- Implementation of early warning systems for potential floods
- Collaboration with local authorities for emergency management
- Monitoring of water quality and biodiversity

### Future Scenarios

With the raising of Lake Maggiore, new possibilities open up for water resource management. In the future, it may be necessary to carefully monitor the water level and take measures to prevent potential problems. However, the current agreement represents an important step towards more sustainable water resource management. For example, the Canton of Ticino is evaluating the implementation of an integrated water resource management system that could include the use of advanced technologies for monitoring and managing water.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Green light for raising Lake Maggiore up to 1.40 meters

For cross-border workers living and working in the Lake Maggiore region, raising the lake's water level could have some practical implications. Here's what to do and what to expect:

### Monitoring the lake level

It's important to monitor the lake level to ensure there are no safety issues. Local authorities will provide regular updates on the water level and any necessary safety measures. For example, the Municipality of Locarno has set up an alert system that sends SMS notifications to residents in case of critical levels. The current lake level is monitored in real-time, and data is available on the **Ticino Canton** website.

### Preparing for potential emergencies

Although the lake's water level increase has been carefully planned, it's always a good idea to be prepared for potential emergencies. Make sure you have an emergency plan in case of flooding or other water-related issues. Here's an operational checklist:

- Identify escape routes: Know the main and secondary escape routes from your neighborhood.
- Prepare an emergency kit: Include drinking water, non-perishable food, a flashlight, battery-powered radio, and important documents.
- Protect your property: Install temporary barriers or sandbags if you live in at-risk areas.

### Participating in local initiatives

Local communities may organize initiatives to promote sustainability and water resource management. Participating in these initiatives can be a way to contribute to the cause and stay informed about the latest news. For example, the Municipality of Brissago has launched an awareness program on water management that includes workshops and the distribution of water-saving kits.

### Using water resources

With the increased availability of water, it may be possible to use water resources more efficiently. This could include garden irrigation, plant maintenance, and other activities that require water. However, it's important to follow local regulations. For example, the 2015 Canton Water Protection Regulation states that water use for irrigation should be limited during drought periods.

### Comparing practical scenarios

- Current scenario: Lake level at 1.20 meters above the average level.
- Future scenario: Lake level at 1.40 meters above the average level.

In case of a rise to 1.40 meters, some areas may be subject to flooding. For example, the Municipality of Ascona has identified some at-risk areas and is working on mitigation projects. Local authorities are also assessing the economic impact, with estimates indicating a cost of around 5 million Swiss francs for protection measures.

### CTA

For more information and to calculate the impact of the lake's water level increase on your specific situation, use our [impact calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/via-libera-allinnalzamento-del-lago-maggiore-fino-a-140-metri-432492)*

item.title: Climate change meetings in Rancio Valcuvia
item.description: The Comunità Montana Valli del Verbano organizes a series of meetings on biodiversity and climate change.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Series of meetings on biodiversity and climate change
- 25 municipalities involved in the Bosco Clima project
- First meeting on April 30 with Adriano Martinoli

## Key facts
- **What**: Series of meetings titled "The changing nature – Challenges and transformations of biodiversity between climate and globalization"
- When: From April to November 2026
- Where: Rancio Valcuvia, municipal hall at piazza Malcotti 1
- Who: Comunità Montana Valli del Verbano, Parco Campo dei Fiori, Università degli Studi dell’Insubria, Centro Geofisico Prealpino, LIPU, CAST, Fondazione Cariplo
- Amount: Not specified

The Comunità Montana Valli del Verbano has launched a series of meetings titled "The changing nature – Challenges and transformations of biodiversity between climate and globalization". The project, which involves 25 municipalities in the area, aims to develop an innovative Climate Transition Strategy. The meetings, six in total, will take place from April to November 2026 and are designed to bring citizens closer to the issues of climate change and the transformations of local ecosystems.

The Bosco Clima project, carried out in collaboration with Parco Campo dei Fiori, Università degli Studi dell’Insubria, Centro Geofisico Prealpino, and the associations LIPU and CAST, with the support of Fondazione Cariplo, focuses on the fundamental role of forests as a key tool to address the challenges of climate change. Forests are considered essential for both adapting to and mitigating climate change.

The first meeting is scheduled for Thursday, April 30, at the municipal hall in piazza Malcotti 1 in Rancio Valcuvia, starting at 8:30 PM. Adriano Martinoli, a professor at Università dell’Insubria, will speak on the topic "The silent war of the squirrels", addressing the phenomenon of alien species and the changes taking place in our forests, with particular attention to the drastic decrease of our red squirrel.

### Calendar and participation
The complete schedule of events is available in the dedicated poster. Participation in the meetings is open to all interested citizens. For more information, you can visit the website dedicated to the Bosco Clima project.

### Implications for cross-border workers
These meetings represent an important opportunity for cross-border workers who live and work in the Valli del Verbano. Understanding climate change and the transformations of local ecosystems can help promote greater environmental awareness and actively participate in the protection of the region's natural heritage. ## Practical Analysis
The cycle of meetings organized by the Comunità Montana Valli del Verbano offers a unique opportunity for citizens and cross-border workers to deepen their knowledge about climate change and local biodiversity. The meetings, which will take place from April to November 2026, cover a wide range of topics, including the silent war of squirrels and the impact of alien species on local ecosystems.

### Scientific Insights
Adriano Martinoli, a professor at the University of Insubria, will be the speaker of the first meeting. His experience and expertise in the field of biodiversity and climate change will provide participants with a detailed overview of current and future challenges. Subsequent meetings, with other experts in the field, will further explore these topics, providing concrete tools to address ecosystem transformations.

### Community Involvement
Participation in the meetings is open to all interested citizens, offering an opportunity for active engagement in environmental protection. Participants will have the chance to ask questions, share their experiences, and contribute to the discussion on how to tackle climate and environmental challenges.

### Impact on the Territory
The Bosco Clima project, with its focus on forests as a key tool to address climate change, has a significant impact on the territory of the Valli del Verbano. Local forests are a natural heritage that requires attention, knowledge, and shared actions. The meetings aim to raise awareness in the community about the importance of protecting and sustainably managing these natural resources.

### Comparison with Other Initiatives
Similar initiatives have been launched in other regions of Italy and Europe, with the aim of promoting environmental awareness and active citizen participation. These projects often involve universities, research centers, and local associations, creating a network of collaboration and knowledge exchange.

### Future Prospects
The meetings organized by the Comunità Montana Valli del Verbano represent just the beginning of a broader path towards greater environmental awareness and sustainable management of natural resources. Participants will have the opportunity to actively contribute to the definition of innovative strategies to address climate and environmental challenges, promoting a more sustainable future for future generations.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions
To participate in the meetings organized by the Comunità Montana Valli del Verbano, you can follow these steps:

1. **Check the calendar**: Visit the dedicated Bosco Clima project website to consult the complete schedule of appointments.
2. Book your spot: Contact the organizational secretariat to book your participation in the meetings of interest.
3. Participate actively: Attend the meetings and contribute to the discussion, asking questions and sharing your experiences.
4. Spread the information: Share the information learned with friends, family, and colleagues to promote greater environmental awareness.

### Useful tools
To further explore the topics discussed during the meetings, you can use the following tools:

- Environmental impact calculator: Use online tools to calculate your environmental impact and identify concrete actions to reduce it.
- Sustainability guides: Consult guides and manuals on environmental sustainability to gain practical knowledge on how to live more sustainably.
- Discussion forums: Participate in online forums and discussion groups to exchange ideas and experiences with other citizens interested in environmental protection.

### Conclusion
The meetings organized by the Comunità Montana Valli del Verbano represent a valuable opportunity for citizens and cross-border workers to deepen their knowledge of climate change and local biodiversity. Actively participating in these meetings and using the available tools can help promote greater environmental awareness and define innovative strategies to address climate and environmental challenges. For more information and to book your participation, visit the dedicated Bosco Clima project website.

[Calculate your environmental impact](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/comunita-montana-valli-del-verbano-promuove-un-ciclo-di-incontri-su-la-natura-che-cambia/2561134/)*

item.title: Webinar on AI in business: strategy and real cases
item.description: The Acinque Group is organizing a webinar on the use of artificial intelligence as a strategic lever for companies.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Webinar on AI organized by Acinque on May 6, 2026
- Analysis of integration costs between processes and governance
- Experts and academics will participate

## Key facts
- **What**: Webinar on the strategic use of AI in companies
- When: Wednesday, May 6, 2026, from 11 AM to 12:30 PM
- Where: Online, through registration at the provided link
- Who: Acinque Group, with the patronage of Confservizi Lombardia
- Fee: Not specified

The webinar 'AI as an enabling factor for achieving the objectives of an industrial plan: strategy and concrete cases' is the fifth and final appointment of the series 'Visions for Tomorrow', organized by the Acinque Group. The event will take place on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, from 11 AM to 12:30 PM, and will delve into the theme of using artificial intelligence as a lever for industrial plans. It will be possible to participate in the webinar by registering at the following link: [https://academy.gruppoacinque.it/](https://academy.gruppoacinque.it/).

### Speakers and presenters

Among the speakers will be Erica Caccamo, Head of Strategy and Innovation at Acinque; Marco Canzi, Vice President of Acinque; Stefano Cetti, CEO of Acinque; Roberto Magnani, cloud & AI expert, member of the Technical-Scientific Committee of ENIA (National Entity for Artificial Intelligence) and AEIT Milan Councilor; Marta Annamaria Tamborini, PhD student in Human Hall (hub for the protection of human rights UNIMI). The event will be moderated by Professor Pierangelo Marucco, who leads the Technical-Scientific Committee of Acinque.

During the round table, the speakers will analyze the impact of generative systems on rights and organizational responsibility, providing leadership with the tools to guide innovation in an ethical, competitive, and sustainable way.

### Webinar objectives

The webinar aims to deepen the challenges of a rapidly evolving reality and intercept new opportunities for entities, businesses, and families. In particular, the real costs of integration between processes and governance will be analyzed, with a focus on how companies are addressing the use of generative models applied to business and internal processes.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers employed in Italian or Swiss companies, this webinar represents an opportunity to better understand how artificial intelligence can influence their sectors. The adoption of advanced technologies can lead to significant changes in business processes, also affecting the required skills and career opportunities.

### Conclusion

The webinar organized by Acinque offers an in-depth analysis of the strategic use of AI in companies, with a focus on integration costs and innovation opportunities. For cross-border workers, participating in this event can be a way to stay updated on market trends and prepare for future changes.

For more information and to register for the webinar, visit the site [https://academy.gruppoacinque.it/](https://academy.gruppoacinque.it/). ### Practical Analysis: Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The adoption of artificial intelligence in companies can have various implications for cross-border workers in sectors such as the economy, technology, and services. In particular, the integration of AI in business processes can lead to greater efficiency and a reduction in operating costs. However, it is important to consider ethical aspects and organizational responsibility as well.

### Comparison Before and After AI Adoption

Before the adoption of AI, many companies relied on manual processes and a larger number of employees to manage operations. With the introduction of AI, it is possible to automate many of these activities, reducing the need for personnel and increasing the precision and speed of processes.

### Concrete Scenarios

A concrete example is the use of AI in managing company data. Companies can use machine learning algorithms to analyze large amounts of data and identify trends and business opportunities. This can lead to greater competitiveness and better resource management.

### Impact on Required Skills

The adoption of AI can also influence the skills required of employees. Companies might seek professionals with advanced technical skills, such as programming and data analysis. For cross-border workers, this means that it may be necessary to invest in continuous training to remain competitive in the job market.

### Final Considerations

In conclusion, the adoption of AI in companies represents both opportunities and challenges for cross-border workers. It is important to stay updated on the latest trends and required skills to adapt to changes in the job market. Participating in webinars like the one organized by Acinque can be an effective way to acquire new knowledge and prepare for the future.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who want to delve into the topic of artificial intelligence and its application in the company, there are several concrete actions they can take. First of all, it is important to register for the webinar organized by Acinque and actively participate in the discussion. This will allow you to acquire new knowledge and network with other professionals in the field.

### Step-by-step procedure

1. **Register for the webinar**: Visit the site [https://academy.gruppoacinque.it/](https://academy.gruppoacinque.it/) and register for the webinar.
2. Prepare your questions: Before the webinar, prepare a list of specific questions you would like to ask the speakers.
3. Participate actively: During the webinar, actively participate in the discussion and take notes on the most relevant information.
4. Follow the speakers: After the webinar, follow the speakers on social media or visit their websites to stay up-to-date on the latest news.
5. Apply the knowledge: Use the knowledge acquired during the webinar to improve your professional skills and adapt to changes in the job market.

### Useful tools

In addition to participating in the webinar, there are several tools and resources that can help cross-border workers stay up-to-date on the latest trends in AI. For example, you can use e-learning platforms like Coursera or Udemy to take specific courses on artificial intelligence and its practical applications. Additionally, it is useful to follow blogs and specialized publications in the field to stay informed about the latest news.

### Final CTA

To further explore the topic and discover how artificial intelligence can influence your work, visit our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and find out how new technologies can improve your career. Additionally, consult our practical guides to stay up-to-date on the latest job market trends and prepare for future changes.

### Conclusion

In conclusion, participating in the webinar organized by Acinque is a valuable opportunity for cross-border workers who want to delve into the topic of artificial intelligence and its application in the company. By following the procedures and using the right tools, it is possible to acquire new skills and prepare for the future of work.

item.title: Lake Maggiore: Italy-Switzerland deal for more spring water
item.description: The maximum level of Lake Maggiore can now reach 1.40 meters to combat the water crisis.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Maximum level of Lake Maggiore raised to 1.40 meters
- Italy-Switzerland agreement to combat water crisis
- More reserves for summer irrigation

## Key facts
- **What**: Raising the maximum level of Lake Maggiore
- When: Decision made on April 29, 2026
- Where: Lake Maggiore, Italy-Switzerland border
- Who: Po River Basin Authority, Piedmont and Lombardy Regions, Ticino Consortium, Italy-Switzerland Bilateral Body
- Amount: Up to 30 million cubic meters of additional water available

After ten years of experimentation, the raising of the maximum regulation level of Lake Maggiore to 1.40 meters above the zero hydrometric level of Sesto Calende becomes a reality. A decision that directly affects the province of Varese, bathed by both Lake Maggiore and the Ticino River, and could guarantee between 20 and 30 million additional cubic meters of water available during the driest periods — with overall volumes that can reach up to five times higher during the irrigation season. The decision was made on April 29 at the end of the Permanent Institutional Conference, the result of an agreement coordinated by the Po River Basin Authority together with the Piedmont and Lombardy Regions, the Ticino Consortium, the Italy-Switzerland Bilateral Body, and numerous stakeholders.

The maximum allowed level will rise from the current 1.25 meters to a range between 1.35 and 1.40 meters, with the possibility of reaching the highest value in case of low snow cover in the Alps, a condition that is becoming increasingly frequent with climate change. The logic is to accumulate water in the spring season to make it available in the summer months, when irrigation demand is higher and rainfall is scarce. For the Varesotto territory, the reflection is direct: the Ticino, which originates from Lake Maggiore at the height of Sesto Calende, feeds an irrigation network that extends towards Lomellina, the Milanese plain, and the lower Pavese area. More reserves in the lake mean more water for those crops — starting with the rice fields, the first rice-growing area in Europe — during the most critical moments of the summer.

### Institutional statements

"Raising the level of Lake Maggiore by up to 15 centimeters is an extraordinary example of how we can and must adapt to climate change," said Alessandro Delpiano, Secretary General of the Po River Basin Authority, emphasizing that the result was achieved "without new works or infrastructures" and "in full respect of hydraulic safety conditions and environmental protection." Satisfaction was also expressed by the Lombardy Region. The assessor for the use of water resources, Massimo Sertori, recalled that Lombardy has been pushing for this experimentation since 2015, and hoped that "at the conclusion of the additional experimental biennium, it will be possible to establish the new levels in an Italo-Swiss agreement" that consolidates the work done. From Piedmont, assessors Matteo Marnati and Paolo Bongioanni spoke of a "concrete sign of support for the agricultural world," guaranteeing at the same time "close monitoring of the areas destined for tourism and bathing" — an aspect relevant also for the Varesotto shores of Lake Maggiore. The president of the Ticino Consortium also expressed satisfaction with the result obtained, emphasizing the urgency of the intervention in light of the current situation: scarcely snow-covered mountain basin and hydroelectric reservoirs at their minimum.

### A cross-border dimension

The story has a dimension that goes beyond national borders. Lake Maggiore is more than half Swiss, and any modification to its regulation requires the involvement of the Swiss Confederation through the Italy-Switzerland Bilateral Body. The fact that the agreement has been reached therefore also represents a diplomatic outcome, as well as a technical one. For the province of Varese — which shares over 80 kilometers of shore with Lake Maggiore and through which the Ticino flows before entering Piedmont — the agreement represents an important step forward in the management of water resources. The agreement reached between Italy and Switzerland to raise the maximum level of Lake Maggiore represents a significant step in the management of water resources, especially in a context of climate change and increasing water crisis. This agreement not only increases the availability of water for summer irrigation but also has important implications for tourism and swimming along the lake's shores.

### Implications for Agriculture

Raising the lake level by up to 1.40 meters allows for the accumulation of larger amounts of water during spring, making it available in the summer months when irrigation demand is highest. This is particularly important for the rice fields of Lomellina, the Milanese plain, and the lower Pavese area, which represent Europe's first rice-growing sector. Greater water reserves mean greater security for crops during drought periods, reducing the risk of economic losses for farmers.

### Impact on Tourism and Swimming

Raising the lake level could also have an impact on tourism and swimming. The Piedmont assessors have guaranteed careful monitoring of the areas designated for these activities, ensuring that the changes do not compromise the safety and quality of tourist experiences. This is particularly relevant for the Varese shores of Lake Maggiore, which attract numerous visitors every year.

### Cross-Border Cooperation

The agreement also represents an example of effective cross-border cooperation. More than half of Lake Maggiore is Swiss, and any modification to its regulation requires the involvement of the Swiss Confederation. The fact that the agreement has been reached demonstrates the ability of the two nations to work together to address common challenges, such as water resource management and adaptation to climate change.

### Conclusions

Raising the maximum level of Lake Maggiore is an important step to address the water crisis and support agriculture and tourism in the region. The Italy-Switzerland agreement represents a model of cross-border cooperation that could be followed in other similar situations.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For cross-border workers and residents along the shores of Lake Maggiore, the agreement reached between Italy and Switzerland to raise the lake's water level represents an opportunity for more sustainable management of water resources. Here's what to do to take advantage of the new provisions and how they might influence daily life. 

### What to do to take advantage of the new provisions

1. Stay informed about the new rules: Make sure you're up-to-date on the new provisions regarding the lake's water level and how they might affect agricultural and tourist activities in your area. You can find updated information on the Po River Basin Authority's website or on the websites of the regions involved.

2. Contact local authorities: If you're a farmer or a tourist operator, contact local authorities to understand how the new provisions might affect your activities and what measures might be necessary to adapt to the changes.

3. Participate in awareness initiatives: Take part in awareness and training initiatives organized by local authorities or industry associations to better understand how to manage water resources sustainably.

### How the new provisions influence daily life

The new provisions might influence daily life in various ways. For example, the rise in the lake's water level could improve water availability for irrigation, reducing the risk of drought and improving crop quality. Moreover, a higher lake level could offer new opportunities for tourism and swimming, attracting more visitors and improving the local economy.

### Useful tools

To delve deeper into the new provisions and understand how they might influence your daily life, you can use various online tools. For example, you can consult the [water resources calculator](nav:calculator) to understand how the new provisions might affect water availability in your area. Additionally, you can use the [water resources comparator](nav:exchange) to compare water availability in different areas of the lake.

In conclusion, the agreement reached between Italy and Switzerland to raise the water level of Lake Maggiore represents an opportunity for more sustainable management of water resources. Take advantage of it to improve your quality of life and contribute to environmental sustainability.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/decisione-storica-sui-livelli-del-lago-maggiore-accordo-italia-svizzera-per-stoccare-piu-acqua-in-primavera-contro-la-crisi-idrica/2561209/)*

item.title: Who funds Swiss politics? Over 130 million in campaigns
item.description: Since transparency rules came into effect, over 130 million francs have been invested in Swiss political campaigns.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Over 130 million in political campaigns since 2023
- Economiesuisse tops the list with nearly 11 million
- APF spends 5.7 million to abolish the rental value

## Key facts
- **What**: Funding for Swiss political campaigns
- When: Since 2023
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Economiesuisse, APF, and other interest groups
- Amount: Over 130 million francs

Since the transparency rules came into effect, over 130 million francs have been invested in Swiss political campaigns. Of these, nearly 61 million come from contributions identified in the Federal Finance Administration's registry. Analyzing these data, nearly 400 different donors have been identified, but a significant portion of the funds comes from a small circle of interest groups.

### Economiesuisse in the lead

One organization stands out above all others: economiesuisse, the umbrella association of the Swiss economy. Very active, it has invested in campaigns related to 16 of the 21 ballot issues analyzed. In total, its declared donations reach nearly 11 million francs, not counting contributions made to political parties outside of campaigns.

'Throughout the year, you have to make choices about the issues, because our resources are not unlimited,' says Cristina Gaggini, director for French-speaking Switzerland at economiesuisse. 'Often, we have to commit decisively to defend the liberal model to which our country owes its success.'

The amounts vary significantly from one issue to another: nearly 3 million francs for the campaign against the 13th AVS pension, compared to just 18,500 francs for the revision of tenancy law. 'We take into account the nature of the issue and its complexity: the more complex it is, the more it needs to be explained, and this requires the mobilization of significant financial resources,' Gaggini specifies.

However, money does not always guarantee a vote's success. Despite the 2.9 million francs invested against the 13th AVS pension, the proposal was accepted by the population. Nevertheless, economiesuisse often finds itself in the winning camp, with ten successes and six defeats since 2024.

### Invisible companies behind organizations

Economiesuisse's funds come from its members, but as for the identity of the companies that contribute the most, Gaggini does not go into detail: 'These are the economic sectors that contribute most to Switzerland's prosperity.' In the official registry, donations are listed under the name of the umbrella organization and not under the name of the companies that finance it.

'This allows very important donors, large companies, not to see their name appear,' explains historian and political scientist Pierre Eichenberger, a member of the Swiss Elites Observatory at the University of Lausanne.

The second most important donor, the Association of Swiss Property Owners (APF), adopts a very different strategy. It has made donations on only five occasions, and the vast majority of the 8.7 million francs invested has been allocated to three issues: the abolition of the rental value and the two revisions of tenancy law. Large donations on rental value

With 5.7 million francs in favor of the abolition of the rental value, the money spent by the APF and its sections represented over 80% of the funds declared for this campaign, considering both sides, for and against. This is the most unbalanced consultation in terms of financial resources among those analyzed, with 92% of the funds going to the campaign for abolition. The massive financial commitment of the AFP was accompanied by a victory, with the issue being accepted with 57% of votes in favor. ### Implications for cross-border workers

Political donations can have a significant impact on cross-border workers in Switzerland, especially in Ticino. Campaigns funded by economiesuisse and APF can influence fiscal and social policies that directly affect transnational workers. For example, the campaign against the 13th AVS pension could influence the AVS and LPP contributions of cross-border workers, while changes to rental law could affect housing costs in Ticino.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the new transparency rules came into effect, political donations were often anonymous. This change has made it possible to identify the main donors and better understand who funds political campaigns. However, as highlighted by Pierre Eichenberger, many donations still go through umbrella organizations, making it difficult to trace the origin of the funds.

### Future scenarios

With the increase in transparency, it is likely that interest groups will continue to play a significant role in Swiss political campaigns. Cross-border workers should carefully monitor campaigns funded by economiesuisse and APF, as these can have a direct impact on their working and living conditions in Switzerland.

### Analysis of donations

The donations from economiesuisse and APF show a clear interest in economic and fiscal policies. Campaigns against the 13th AVS pension and for the abolition of the rental value reflect a targeted strategy to influence policies that can have a significant impact on the Swiss economy and cross-border workers.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ### What cross-border workers should do

Cross-border workers employed in Switzerland should closely monitor the political campaigns funded by economiesuisse and APF. It's important to understand how these campaigns can influence the fiscal and social policies that directly affect transborder workers. For example, changes to tenancy law could impact housing costs in Ticino, while campaigns against the 13th AVS pension could affect AVS and LPP contributions.

### Procedure to monitor campaigns

1. **Inform yourself**: Read articles and reports on the political campaigns funded by economiesuisse and APF.
2. Participate: Take part in public consultations and votes concerning fiscal and social policies.
3. Monitor: Keep track of political decisions that can influence the working and living conditions of cross-border workers in Switzerland.
4. Use tools: Utilize tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to better understand the impact of fiscal policies on your income.

### Conclusions

Political donations in Switzerland have a significant impact on cross-border workers in Ticino. It's important to closely monitor the campaigns funded by economiesuisse and APF and understand how these can influence fiscal and social policies. Using tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help better understand the impact of fiscal policies on your income.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3702801)*

item.title: Switzerland-Serbia 2026-29 cooperation program
item.description: President Guy Parmelin presents a new cooperation program with Serbia, focusing on economy and innovation.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Switzerland and Serbia launch 2026-29 cooperation program
- Focus on economy and innovation
- Bilateral agreement for innovation

## Key facts
- **What**: New 2026-29 cooperation program
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Serbia
- Who: President Guy Parmelin and his Serbian counterpart Aleksandar Vučić
- Amount: Trade exchanges worth approximately one billion francs

On the occasion of the 110th anniversary of bilateral relations, Swiss Confederation President Guy Parmelin presented a new cooperation program for the period 2026-29 in Serbia. The program, focused on democratic good governance, economic development, strengthening basic vocational training, climate change, and sustainable urban development, supports Serbia in the European integration process. This is what emerges from a statement released Tuesday by the Swiss Federal Department of Economic Affairs, Education and Research (DEFR).

During the visit, Parmelin met with Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić and Prime Minister Đuro Macut. The meeting also saw the signing of an agreement between the Swiss State Secretariat for Economic Affairs (SECO) and the Serbian Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (NITRA) for a bilateral innovation program. This agreement aims to accelerate market access for new products and services, strengthen collaboration between the private sector and research, and initiate regular dialogue on human rights.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The new cooperation program could have positive implications for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Serbia. In particular, the strengthening of basic vocational training and support for economic development could create new job opportunities and improve living conditions for cross-border workers. Furthermore, the bilateral innovation program could foster the creation of new businesses and economic growth, with indirect benefits for cross-border workers.

President Parmelin emphasized the importance of stability and prosperity in the Western Balkans, recognizing the progress made in the region in terms of economy and security policy. He also reiterated Switzerland's willingness to engage in favor of dialogue between Belgrade and Kosovo, a state with which a cooperation program with very similar terms has been signed.

### The Serbian diaspora in Switzerland

Parmelin's visit will continue on Wednesday with the first presidential visit to North Macedonia. The Serbian diaspora in Switzerland counts approximately 130,000 representatives, a significant number that underscores the importance of bilateral relations between the two countries. Serbia remains Switzerland's main economic partner in the Western Balkans, with a trade exchange volume of approximately one billion francs. ## Analysis of Opportunities

The new cooperation program between Switzerland and Serbia could open new opportunities for cross-border workers in Ticino. In particular, the strengthening of basic vocational training could improve workers' skills and increase employment opportunities. Furthermore, support for economic development could foster the creation of new businesses and economic growth, with indirect benefits for cross-border workers.

### Implications for the Canton of Ticino

For cross-border workers in Ticino, the new cooperation program could mean more job opportunities and better living conditions. In particular, the strengthening of basic vocational training could improve workers' skills and increase employment opportunities. Furthermore, support for economic development could foster the creation of new businesses and economic growth, with indirect benefits for cross-border workers.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the new cooperation program, cross-border workers in Ticino residing in Serbia could face some challenges, such as the lack of vocational training opportunities and the difficulty of finding work. With the new program, these challenges could be overcome, improving living and working conditions for cross-border workers.

### Future Scenarios

If the cooperation program is successful, it could lead to greater economic integration between Switzerland and Serbia, with benefits for cross-border workers. In particular, the strengthening of basic vocational training could improve workers' skills and increase employment opportunities. Furthermore, support for economic development could foster the creation of new businesses and economic growth, with indirect benefits for cross-border workers.

### Conclusion

The new cooperation program between Switzerland and Serbia represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers in Ticino. With the strengthening of basic vocational training and support for economic development, cross-border workers could benefit from more job opportunities and better living conditions. Furthermore, the bilateral innovation program could foster the creation of new businesses and economic growth, with indirect benefits for cross-border workers.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

For cross-border workers employed in Ticino and residing in Serbia, it is important to monitor the developments of the new cooperation program. In particular, it is useful to stay updated on professional training opportunities and economic development initiatives. Additionally, it is advisable to participate in bilateral innovation programs to improve skills and increase employment opportunities.

### Concrete steps

1. **Inform yourself about training opportunities**: Contact the competent offices in Switzerland and Serbia to obtain information on professional training opportunities.
2. Participate in innovation programs: Register for bilateral innovation programs to improve your skills and increase employment opportunities.
3. Monitor economic developments: Stay updated on economic development initiatives and job opportunities.
4. Use the site's tools: Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job opportunities and plan your future.

### Conclusion

The new cooperation program between Switzerland and Serbia represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers in Ticino. With the strengthening of basic professional training and support for economic development, cross-border workers could benefit from more job opportunities and better living conditions. Furthermore, the bilateral innovation program could foster the creation of new businesses and economic growth, with indirect benefits for cross-border workers.

Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job opportunities and plan your future.

item.title: Italian research in Switzerland: focus on life sciences
item.description: Geneva hosts the Italian Research Day in the World, with a focus on Italo-Swiss scientific cooperation
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Italian researchers in Switzerland gather in Geneva
- Focus on life sciences and Italo-Swiss collaboration
- Event organized at Bracco in Plan-les-Ouates

## Key facts
- **What**: Italian Research Day in the World
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Plan-les-Ouates, Geneva
- Who: Italian researchers in Switzerland
- Attendance: Around 60 participants

Geneva hosted on Tuesday, April 29, 2026, the ninth edition of the Italian Research Day in the World (GRIM). The event, which takes place annually around April 22, the anniversary of the birth of Rita Levi-Montalcini, Nobel Prize winner in medicine, brought together around 60 Italian researchers active in Switzerland. The initiative, organized at the Bracco site in Plan-les-Ouates, in the canton of Geneva, focused on life sciences and opportunities for collaboration between the Italian and Swiss scientific and health systems.

### Italo-Swiss scientific cooperation

The GRIM highlights the contribution of the Italian scientific community abroad and the dialogue with the host countries. During the event, examples of applied research in the medical field were presented, with particular attention to the transfer of scientific results to clinical practice and patient care. The importance of stable transnational networks emerged, which allow addressing complex challenges in life sciences.
